text:
# Carcavelos Forecast – Saturday 23 May

**Tiny but clean: a mellow spring day for the early birds.**

Morning offers the most organised conditions with 1.4m waves and light NNW winds keeping things glassy until 08:00. **Best window: 05:00–08:00** when offshore flow and consistent 8.4s+ swell create your cleanest peaks. Wind turns onshore from the NE around 09:00, gradually diminishing swell quality through mid-morning.

Afternoon sees the swell dwindle to 1.2m as winds swing SE and conditions become mushy. Water temps climb to 26°C—beautiful but frustratingly small by lunchtime.

**Tips:** Grab a shortboard or fish and go early—the 5am slot is your money time. Crowds should be minimal on a Saturday morning, so you'll have space. If you sleep in, don't bother after 10:00; the beach will be packed with swimmers and the waves will be closing out.
